analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet, quoting a Chinese MFA spokesperson, condemns US diplomacy as relying on intimidation, lies, and sanctions, and asserts it is arrogant for a young country (the US) to think it can change a major country with a 4,000-year political civilization (China). Evaluation: CH1 (The West is immoral, hostile and decadent) — Yes. The tweet portrays the US (the West) as engaging in intimidation, lies, and sanctions, casting it as hostile and immoral, with China as the target of such behavior. CH2 (China is a benevolent power) — No. There is no content highlighting China’s cooperative or benevolent international stance. CH3 (China has an epic history) — Partial elements but primarily used as contrast; still, it explicitly invokes China’s 4,000-year political civilization to assert endurance and depth, which aligns with showcasing China’s long, resilient history. Thus, CH3 applies. CH4 (China has an appropriate political system and honorable values) — Not explicitly. While the tweet contrasts civilizations, it does not advocate China’s domestic political system or values as superior in governance terms. CH5 (The government of the CCP succeeds) — No mention of governance achievements or development successes. CH6 (China is interesting culturally) — Not applicable. Conclusion: The tweet promotes CH1 and CH3.
